plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-290 RP08.P06.M09.S03-RP08.P07.M05.S04 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-290 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP08.P06 permission phase (M09 Claude review packet tail, M10 closeout permission cycle) and opens the RP08.P07 failure-handling foundation (M00-M05: failure taxonomies, missing tenant/actor/Matter/resource failures, unknown action/cross-tenant/permission-denied/ambiguous-rule/stale-reference failures, lock conflict and retry exhaustion failures, rollback/compensation expectations, blocked-claim receipts, failure fixtures/tests, audit failure hints, Hermes failure evidence, Claude edge-case prompts, human escalation notes) across eight sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H08), Claude review packet (C08), and CP00-291 handoff, without opening rollback/retry runtime, lock acquisition, runtime permission evaluation, audit event writes, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary:
CP00-290 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (Risk C, 150 units, RP08.P06.M09.S03-RP08.P07.M05.S04) closing the RP08.P06 permission phase (M09 Claude-review-packet tail, M10 closeout permission cycle) and opening the RP08.P07 failure-handling foundation (M00-M05 head). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the claims are honest and internally consistent across registry.js, service.js, validators.js, the contract projection, model.test.js, README, and manifest.json.

BOUNDARIES: Every changed surface is descriptor-only. The service.js freeze function (freezeCp290Result) and the contract projection assert synthetic_only/no_real_data=true and writes_product_state=false, with all runtime dispatch, DB create/update, object-storage, lock, retry/rollback, audit-write, and runtime-permission-evaluation flags false. Descriptor builders only construct frozen objects; no product runtime code is introduced.

DISTRIBUTION (independently recounted from the baseline included_units): eight deliverable counts (implementation 23, security_audit 19, ui 8, claude_review 5, test 16, failure_recovery 64, hermes_evidence 10, fixture 5 = 150), two phase counts (P06 40 / P07 110), and eight micro-title counts all match the prompt distribution and the registry requirements; validators enforce each via distribution-drift checks.

AUTHORITY BOUNDARY: claude_is_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false in both the no_write_attestation and the descriptor authority_boundary; promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; review-request is read_only with only Read/Grep/Glob. Claude remains an independent reviewer, not the final approver; human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true (runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-291).

PRODUCTION_READY POSTURE IS HONEST: manifest and command-evidence report production_ready=false (status implemented_pending_review). Only production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && foundation.valid) is true, which is appropriate for a descriptor-only foundation pack still pending human final approval and the full review/adjudication/inspection gate sequence described in the governance handoff. No overstatement of readiness.

HERMES: emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false and receipt_shape is descriptor_only_..._no_write; Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor-only evidence-packet/receipt handling (H08), not product runtime, consistent with hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false.

CP178+ REVIEW HARDENING: the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid, non-zero JSON (no auth failure, no 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output); the contract's invalid_review_blockers enumerate all required rejection categories (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, disabled_tools_run, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim). changed-file-scope reports active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted: [] and validation-report confirms forbidden_tools_present=false.

No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One P3 commit-hygiene observation only (below). Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Unrelated untracked files (incl. a BLOCK-verdict LDIP review artifact) sit in the worktree alongside CP00-290 files
findings.details: The worktree contains untracked files that belong to other workstreams, not CP00-290: docs/ldip-integration/claude-review-results/c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json (itself a read-only BLOCK verdict from a separate LDIP planning track), a stale docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md (snapshot dated 2026-06-08 referencing CP00-082 as latest, explicitly self-described as possibly stale), and a v2.0 .docx. changed-file-scope.json correctly classifies all of these under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and lists active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted: [], so none are treated as CP00-290 deliverables. Two minor notes: (1) new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-290/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved in the scope file, which is slightly ambiguous but harmless because the pack's real deliverables are the 8 tracked-modified implementation refs plus docs/closeout-packs/cp00-290/manifest.json and command-evidence.json; (2) the LDIP artifact asserts a <system-reminder> prompt-injection block at new-session-handoff.md lines 51-52, but the current file is clean at those lines (a benign source-file list) and is out of CP00-290 scope. Risk is limited to an over-broad `git add` accidentally sweeping these unrelated files into the CP00-290 closeout commit.
findings.recommendation: When committing CP00-290, stage only pack paths (the 8 active_pack_implementation_refs plus docs/closeout-packs/cp00-290/ and the review artifacts) and rely on the already-planned post-review `git diff --check --cached` gate to confirm no unrelated files are staged. Optionally tighten changed-file-scope so files are not listed in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational. Already mitigated by changed-file-scope segregation (active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted: []) and the planned `git diff --check --cached` gate. Does not affect the descriptor-only boundary, the authority boundary, or the production_ready posture; does not block pack or goal closeout.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-290 email dms core p06 closeout p07 failure foundation descriptor pack
